meta.description: Compressed hunt cards for oracle — one-line detection rules for bulk scanning
meta.usage: Load cards into context. For each card, grep target code for card.grep. On hit: execute card.check steps directly, use validWhen/invalidWhen/impact for rapid triage, and only read full DB entry (card.ref + card.lines) for confirmed true/likely positives.

cards.title: 1. CheckUpkeep/PerformUpkeep Mismatch
cards.detect: `checkUpkeep` returns data encoded in one format, but `performUpkeep` expects a different format.
check: VERIFY: checkUpkeep returns data encoded in one format, but performUpkeep expects a different format.
PROVE: Protocol registers Chainlink Automation upkeep; checkUpkeep returns upkeepNeeded = true with encoded data
FALSIFY: Matching encode/decode formats
IMPACT: Automation completely non-functional
Verify checkUpkeep return type matches performUpkeep decode
Check that performData is actually used (not recalculated)
cards.antipattern: checkUpkeep returns uint256, performUpkeep expects bytes[]
cards.validWhen: checkUpkeep returns data encoded in one format, but performUpkeep expects a different format.

cards.title: 2. Gas Limit Issues
cards.detect: `performUpkeep` contains unbounded loops, multiple external calls, or complex operations that can exceed the registered...
check: VERIFY: performUpkeep contains unbounded loops, multiple external calls, or complex operations that can exceed the registered gas limit.
PROVE: Loop over all vaults can exceed gas limit
FALSIFY: Process in bounded batches
cards.antipattern: Loop over all vaults can exceed gas limit
cards.validWhen: performUpkeep contains unbounded loops, multiple external calls, or complex operations that can exceed the registered gas limit.

cards.title: 3. Unbounded Operations
cards.detect: All logs in block must be processed
check: VERIFY: All logs in block must be processed
FALSIFY: Track and batch process
cards.antipattern: All logs in block must be processed
cards.validWhen: All logs in block must be processed

cards.title: 4. Callback Safety
cards.detect: Strict requirements can cause repeated failures
check: VERIFY: Strict requirements can cause repeated failures
FALSIFY: Handle edge cases gracefully
cards.antipattern: Strict requirements can cause repeated failures
cards.validWhen: Strict requirements can cause repeated failures

cards.title: 5. Authentication Missing
cards.detect: Functions source code contains hardcoded API endpoints without authentication, making them publicly callable and...
check: VERIFY: Functions source code contains hardcoded API endpoints without authentication, making them publicly callable and vulnerable to abuse.
PROVE: No authentication on API call
FALSIFY: Use encrypted secrets for authentication
IMPACT: API endpoint exposed publicly
cards.antipattern: No authentication on API call
cards.validWhen: Functions source code contains hardcoded API endpoints without authentication, making them publicly callable and vulnerable to abuse.

cards.title: 6. Response Validation
cards.detect: Blindly trusts response
check: VERIFY: Blindly trusts response
FALSIFY: Validate response thoroughly
cards.antipattern: Blindly trusts response
cards.validWhen: Blindly trusts response

cards.title: 2. Router Configuration Issues
cards.detect: Router address stored as immutable or constant without update mechanism.
check: VERIFY: Router address stored as immutable or constant without update mechanism.
PROVE: Cannot update if Chainlink changes router
FALSIFY: Following Chainlink's recommended pattern
cards.antipattern: Cannot update if Chainlink changes router
cards.validWhen: Router address stored as immutable or constant without update mechanism.

cards.title: 3. Extra Args Configuration
cards.detect: `extraArgs` hardcoded in code rather than passed as parameters or stored in upgradeable storage.
check: VERIFY: extraArgs hardcoded in code rather than passed as parameters or stored in upgradeable storage.
PROVE: Hardcoded, cannot adapt to changes
FALSIFY: extraArgs is configurable
cards.antipattern: Hardcoded, cannot adapt to changes
cards.validWhen: extraArgs hardcoded in code rather than passed as parameters or stored in upgradeable storage.

cards.title: 4. Source/Sender Validation
cards.detect: `_ccipReceive` processes messages without validating the source chain selector or sender address, allowing malicious...
check: VERIFY: _ccipReceive processes messages without validating the source chain selector or sender address, allowing malicious actors to send fake cross-chain...
PROVE: Accepts messages from any source
FALSIFY: Validate both chain and sender
cards.antipattern: Accepts messages from any source
cards.validWhen: _ccipReceive processes messages without validating the source chain selector or sender address, allowing malicious actors to send fake cross-chain messages.

cards.title: 5. Callback/Receive Handling
cards.detect: Complex logic, external calls, or state validations in `_ccipReceive` that can fail, causing permanent message loss.
check: VERIFY: Complex logic, external calls, or state validations in _ccipReceive that can fail, causing permanent message loss.
PROVE: State validation can fail
FALSIFY: Store for later processing
cards.antipattern: State validation can fail
cards.validWhen: Complex logic, external calls, or state validations in _ccipReceive that can fail, causing permanent message loss.

cards.title: 6. Gas Limit Configuration
cards.detect: Default might not be enough
check: VERIFY: Default might not be enough
FALSIFY: Appropriate gas limit for operation
cards.antipattern: Default might not be enough
cards.validWhen: Default might not be enough

cards.title: Vulnerable Pattern Examples
cards.detect: No validation of updatedAt timestamp
check: VERIFY: No validation of updatedAt timestamp
PROVE: Same threshold for all feeds (24 hours)
cards.antipattern: Same threshold for all feeds (24 hours)
cards.validWhen: No validation of updatedAt timestamp

cards.title: 4. Deprecated API Usage
cards.detect: `latestAnswer()` only returns the price without timestamps or round information, making it impossible to validate...
check: VERIFY: latestAnswer() only returns the price without timestamps or round information, making it impossible to validate staleness or round completeness.
PROVE: answeredInRound is being deprecated
FALSIFY: Use modern API with proper validation
cards.antipattern: answeredInRound is being deprecated
cards.securePattern: Use modern API with proper validation
cards.validWhen: latestAnswer() only returns the price without timestamps or round information, making it impossible to validate staleness or round completeness.
cards.invalidWhen: Use modern API with proper validation

cards.title: 5. Access Denial / Revert Handling
cards.detect: Direct calls to `latestRoundData()` without try/catch.
check: VERIFY: Direct calls to latestRoundData() without try/catch.
PROVE: Unhandled revert can DOS entire protocol
FALSIFY: Graceful handling with fallback oracle
cards.antipattern: Unhandled revert can DOS entire protocol
cards.validWhen: Direct calls to latestRoundData() without try/catch.

cards.title: 6. Decimal/Precision Handling
cards.detect: Hardcoded assumption that all Chainlink feeds use 8 decimals.
check: VERIFY: Hardcoded assumption that all Chainlink feeds use 8 decimals.
PROVE: Assumes all feeds have 8 decimals
FALSIFY: Properly scale based on actual feed decimals
cards.antipattern: Assumes all feeds have 8 decimals
cards.securePattern: Properly scale based on actual feed decimals
cards.validWhen: Hardcoded assumption that all Chainlink feeds use 8 decimals.
cards.invalidWhen: Properly scale based on actual feed decimals

cards.title: 7. Phase ID / Round ID Handling
cards.detect: Protocols that query historical rounds using `roundId - 1` will fail after a phase change because that round doesn't...
check: VERIFY: Protocols that query historical rounds using roundId - 1 will fail after a phase change because that round doesn't exist in the new phase.
PROVE: Assumes rounds are sequential
FALSIFY: Handle phase transitions gracefully
cards.antipattern: Assumes rounds are sequential
cards.validWhen: Protocols that query historical rounds using roundId - 1 will fail after a phase change because that round doesn't exist in the new phase.

cards.title: 8. Price Manipulation / Front-Running
cards.detect: Predictable oracle updates allow MEV bots to sandwich transactions or front-run price changes for profit.
check: VERIFY: Predictable oracle updates allow MEV bots to sandwich transactions or front-run price changes for profit.
Review if protocol operations can be sandwiched around oracle updates
Check for commit-reveal patterns to prevent front-running
cards.validWhen: Predictable oracle updates allow MEV bots to sandwich transactions or front-run price changes for profit.

cards.title: 1. VRF Re-Roll / Manipulation Attacks
cards.detect: When VRF request and fulfillment happen in quick succession without sufficient block confirmations, miners can...
check: VERIFY: When VRF request and fulfillment happen in quick succession without sufficient block confirmations, miners can reorganize the chain to produce...
PROVE: Protocol requests VRF randomness; Miner/validator sees the VRF fulfillment transaction in mempool
FALSIFY: Commit-reveal with time delay
IMPACT: Randomness can be biased by block producers
Verify REQUEST_CONFIRMATIONS >= 3 (higher for mainnet)
Check for commit-reveal patterns in high-stakes scenarios
cards.antipattern: Low confirmation count allows re-org attacks
cards.validWhen: When VRF request and fulfillment happen in quick succession without sufficient block confirmations, miners can reorganize the chain to produce different randomness...
cards.impact: Randomness can be biased by block producers

cards.title: 3. Callback Revert Issues
cards.detect: Complex logic, external calls, or state validations in `fulfillRandomWords` that can fail under certain conditions.
check: VERIFY: Complex logic, external calls, or state validations in fulfillRandomWords that can fail under certain conditions.
PROVE: Protocol requests VRF randomness; Chainlink provides randomness callback
FALSIFY: Store randomness, process separately
IMPACT: Lost randomness that cannot be recovered
Verify fulfillRandomWords has minimal logic
Check no external calls that can fail
cards.antipattern: External call can fail
cards.validWhen: Complex logic, external calls, or state validations in fulfillRandomWords that can fail under certain conditions.
cards.impact: Lost randomness that cannot be recovered

cards.title: 4. VRF Version Deprecation
cards.detect: VRF V1 has security limitations and is no longer maintained.
check: VERIFY: VRF V1 has security limitations and is no longer maintained.
PROVE: Using deprecated VRF V1
cards.antipattern: Using deprecated VRF V1
cards.validWhen: VRF V1 has security limitations and is no longer maintained.

cards.title: 5. Weak Randomness Sources
cards.detect: Predictable randomness
check: VERIFY: Predictable randomness
PROVE: Can be predicted/manipulated
FALSIFY: True randomness from VRF
cards.antipattern: Can be predicted/manipulated
cards.validWhen: Predictable randomness

cards.title: 6. Request Configuration Issues
cards.detect: Below Chainlink minimum (3)
check: VERIFY: Below Chainlink minimum (3)
PROVE: Gas too low for callback logic
FALSIFY: Proper VRF configuration
cards.antipattern: Gas too low for callback logic
cards.validWhen: Below Chainlink minimum (3)

cards.title: Pattern 1: Flash Swap Reserve Drain → Inflated Reward Claims
cards.detect: Severity**: 🔴 CRITICAL | **Loss**: ~$127K | **Protocol**: ATK (Journey of Awakening) | **Chain**: BSC
check: VERIFY: Severity: 🔴 CRITICAL | Loss: ~$127K | Protocol: ATK (Journey of Awakening) | Chain: BSC

cards.title: Pattern 2: Multi-function Reward Drain via Pair Price Manipulation
cards.detect: Severity**: 🟠 HIGH | **Loss**: USDT profit | **Protocol**: YYDS | **Chain**: BSC
check: VERIFY: Severity: 🟠 HIGH | Loss: USDT profit | Protocol: YYDS | Chain: BSC

cards.title: Pattern 3: batchToken Mint Directly Into LP Pair
cards.detect: Severity**: 🔴 CRITICAL | **Loss**: ~$3M | **Protocol**: ZoomproFinance | **Chain**: BSC
check: VERIFY: Severity: 🔴 CRITICAL | Loss: ~$3M | Protocol: ZoomproFinance | Chain: BSC

cards.title: Pattern 4: Public Token Function Skews LP Reserves
cards.detect: Severity**: 🟠 HIGH | **Loss**: ~$25K | **Protocol**: SpaceGodzilla | **Chain**: BSC
check: VERIFY: Severity: 🟠 HIGH | Loss: ~$25K | Protocol: SpaceGodzilla | Chain: BSC

cards.title: Pattern 5: Broken transferFrom Drains LP Pair Reserves
cards.detect: Severity**: 🔴 CRITICAL | **Loss**: ~17 WBNB | **Protocol**: NOVO | **Chain**: BSC
check: VERIFY: Severity: 🔴 CRITICAL | Loss: ~17 WBNB | Protocol: NOVO | Chain: BSC

cards.title: Pattern 6: Flash Loan Vault Share Inflation
cards.detect: Severity**: 🔴 CRITICAL | **Loss**: ~$2M | **Protocol**: OneRing Finance | **Chain**: Fantom
check: VERIFY: Severity: 🔴 CRITICAL | Loss: ~$2M | Protocol: OneRing Finance | Chain: Fantom

cards.title: Pattern 7: Spot Price Swap Proxy Arbitrage
cards.detect: Severity**: 🔴 CRITICAL | **Loss**: ~$500K | **Protocol**: APC | **Chain**: BSC
check: VERIFY: Severity: 🔴 CRITICAL | Loss: ~$500K | Protocol: APC | Chain: BSC

cards.title: Pattern 8: balanceOf-Based Reward Recycling Across Contracts
cards.detect: Severity**: 🔴 CRITICAL | **Loss**: ~$1.25M (4,481 BNB) | **Protocol**: NewFreeDAO (NFD) | **Chain**: BSC
check: VERIFY: Severity: 🔴 CRITICAL | Loss: ~$1.25M (4,481 BNB) | Protocol: NewFreeDAO (NFD) | Chain: BSC

cards.title: Invariant Checks
cards.detect: INV-ORACLE-001: Token price must not change >10% within a single block
check: VERIFY: INV-ORACLE-001: Token price must not change >10% within a single block
cards.validWhen: INV-ORACLE-001: Token price must not change >10% within a single block

cards.title: 1. AMM Reserve-Based Price Oracle — Direct LP Pool Manipulation
cards.detect: This vulnerability exists because the price calculation function reads **real-time AMM pool reserve balances** to...
check: VERIFY: price calculation function reads real-time AMM pool reserve balances to derive token prices.
PROVE: Attacker flash-borrows the majority of the quote token from the target LP pool; During the flash callback, the pool's reserve ratio is distorted
ANTIPATTERN: Stablecoin mint() and redeem() use AMM spot price
cards.antipattern: Stablecoin mint() and redeem() use AMM spot price
cards.validWhen: This vulnerability exists because the price calculation function reads real-time AMM pool reserve balances to derive token prices.

cards.title: 2. Curve LP / Virtual Price Oracle Manipulation
cards.detect: This vulnerability exists because lending protocols use Curve pool's **internal state** (virtual_price, LP token value)...
check: VERIFY: lending protocols use Curve pool's internal state (virtual_price, LP token value) as a price oracle for collateral.
PROVE: Flash loan massive amounts of assets that Curve pools accept; Perform a large swap on the Curve Tricrypto or other pool to distort virtual_price
ANTIPATTERN: Oracle derives price from Curve Tricrypto pool state
cards.antipattern: Oracle derives price from Curve Tricrypto pool state
cards.validWhen: This vulnerability exists because lending protocols use Curve pool's internal state (virtual_price, LP token value) as a price oracle for collateral.

cards.title: 3. Solidly LP Oracle + Weak Signature Validation
cards.detect: This vulnerability exists because the lending protocol uses a Solidly/Solidex LP token as collateral with an oracle...
check: VERIFY: lending protocol uses a Solidly/Solidex LP token as collateral with an oracle that reads LP value from manipulable on-chain state,...
PROVE: Lending uses Solidly LP with manipulable oracle
cards.antipattern: Lending uses Solidly LP with manipulable oracle
cards.validWhen: This vulnerability exists because the lending protocol uses a Solidly/Solidex LP token as collateral with an oracle that reads LP value from manipulable on-chain state,...

cards.title: 4. Vault Share Price Inflation via Donation
cards.detect: This vulnerability exists because lending protocols accept vault tokens (plvGLP, yearn vault shares) as collateral and...
check: VERIFY: lending protocols accept vault tokens (plvGLP, yearn vault shares) as collateral and price them based on the vault's exchange rate...
PROVE: Lending market prices plvGLP by vault exchange rate
cards.antipattern: Lending market prices plvGLP by vault exchange rate
cards.validWhen: This vulnerability exists because lending protocols accept vault tokens (plvGLP, yearn vault shares) as collateral and price them based on the vault's exchange rate...

cards.title: 1. Low-Cost Oracle Reporter Manipulation (BonqDAO $88M)
cards.detect: BonqDAO used TellorFlex oracle for wALBT token pricing.
check: VERIFY: BonqDAO used TellorFlex oracle for wALBT token pricing.
PROVE: *Tx1 — Borrow at inflated price; Stake 10 TRB on TellorFlex
ANTIPATTERN: Protocol reads latest Tellor price without dispute window
cards.antipattern: Protocol reads latest Tellor price without dispute window
cards.validWhen: BonqDAO used TellorFlex oracle for wALBT token pricing.

cards.title: 2. Concentrated Liquidity Bin Manipulation (Jimbo $8M)
cards.detect: Jimbo Protocol used a custom liquidity distribution mechanism on Trader Joe V2.1 (Liquidity Book).
check: VERIFY: Jimbo Protocol used a custom liquidity distribution mechanism on Trader Joe V2.1 (Liquidity Book).
PROVE: shift() redistributes liquidity based on current bin price
cards.antipattern: shift() redistributes liquidity based on current bin price
cards.validWhen: Jimbo Protocol used a custom liquidity distribution mechanism on Trader Joe V2.1 (Liquidity Book).

cards.title: 3. Curve LP Token Price Manipulation (Zunami $2M)
cards.detect: Zunami Protocol used Curve LP tokens (sETH/ETH, frxETH/ETH) whose price was derived from pool reserves.
check: VERIFY: Zunami Protocol used Curve LP tokens (sETH/ETH, frxETH/ETH) whose price was derived from pool reserves.
PROVE: Zunami oracle reads Curve LP price from spot reserves
cards.antipattern: Zunami oracle reads Curve LP price from spot reserves
cards.validWhen: Zunami Protocol used Curve LP tokens (sETH/ETH, frxETH/ETH) whose price was derived from pool reserves.

cards.title: 4. vToken Collateral Oracle Manipulation (0vix $2M)
cards.detect: 0vix Protocol (Compound V2 fork on Polygon) used vGHST as a collateral token.
check: VERIFY: 0vix Protocol (Compound V2 fork on Polygon) used vGHST as a collateral token.
PROVE: vGHST price derived from aGHST vault exchange rate
cards.antipattern: vGHST price derived from aGHST vault exchange rate
cards.validWhen: 0vix Protocol (Compound V2 fork on Polygon) used vGHST as a collateral token.

cards.title: 5. Flash Loan + Spot Price (CompounderFinance $27.2M, Gamma $6.3M, Allbridge $550K)
cards.detect: Multiple protocols in 2023 continued to use DEX spot prices (pair reserves, `getReserves()`) as oracle prices.
check: VERIFY: Multiple protocols in 2023 continued to use DEX spot prices (pair reserves, getReserves()) as oracle prices.
PROVE: Protocol uses spot DEX price for valuation
cards.antipattern: Protocol uses spot DEX price for valuation
cards.validWhen: Multiple protocols in 2023 continued to use DEX spot prices (pair reserves, getReserves()) as oracle prices.

cards.title: 6. Lending Protocol Oracle Manipulation (RodeoFinance $888K)
cards.detect: RodeoFinance on Arbitrum used a TWAP oracle for collateral pricing, but the TWAP window was too short (1 hour or less).
check: VERIFY: RodeoFinance on Arbitrum used a TWAP oracle for collateral pricing, but the TWAP window was too short (1 hour or less).
PROVE: TWAP window too short — manipulable over a single hour
cards.antipattern: TWAP window too short — manipulable over a single hour
cards.validWhen: RodeoFinance on Arbitrum used a TWAP oracle for collateral pricing, but the TWAP window was too short (1 hour or less).

cards.title: Secure Implementations
cards.detect: // ✅ SECURE: Read Tellor price with sufficient dispute window
check: VERIFY: // SECURE: Read Tellor price with sufficient dispute window
FALSIFY: Read Tellor price with sufficient dispute window
cards.securePattern: Read Tellor price with sufficient dispute window
cards.validWhen: // SECURE: Read Tellor price with sufficient dispute window
cards.invalidWhen: Read Tellor price with sufficient dispute window

cards.title: 1. Curve Pool-Based Oracle Manipulation for Lending Liquidation
cards.detect: Lending protocols that derive collateral/debt asset prices from Curve pool spot prices are vulnerable to...
check: VERIFY: Lending protocols that derive collateral/debt asset prices from Curve pool spot prices are vulnerable to flash-loan-driven price manipulation.
PROVE: Flash loan ~$1.1B across 6+ lending protocols (Aave V3, V2, Spark, Morpho, etc.); Dump target asset (e.g., USDe) across 5 Curve pools → depresses sUSDE price
ANTIPATTERN: Lending protocol using Curve pool spot prices for sUSDE valuation
cards.antipattern: Lending protocol using Curve pool spot prices for sUSDE valuation
cards.validWhen: Lending protocols that derive collateral/debt asset prices from Curve pool spot prices are vulnerable to flash-loan-driven price manipulation.

cards.title: 2. Self-Referencing Oracle (sPMM) Manipulation
cards.detect: Proactive market maker (PMM) DEXes like WooPPV2 use an internal oracle (WooracleV2) that updates its price state based...
check: VERIFY: Proactive market maker (PMM) DEXes like WooPPV2 use an internal oracle (WooracleV2) that updates its price state based on swaps executed through the...
PROVE: WooPPV2 oracle updates from its own swaps
cards.antipattern: WooPPV2 oracle updates from its own swaps
cards.validWhen: Proactive market maker (PMM) DEXes like WooPPV2 use an internal oracle (WooracleV2) that updates its price state based on swaps executed through the same pool.

cards.title: 3. Concentrated Liquidity Vault Deposit/Withdraw Cycling
cards.detect: Concentrated liquidity vaults (e.
check: VERIFY: Concentrated liquidity vaults (e.g., Gamma on Algebra/Uniswap V3) calculate LP share amounts based on the current spot price of the underlying pool.
PROVE: Gamma UniProxy.deposit() uses spot price for share calculation
cards.antipattern: Gamma UniProxy.deposit() uses spot price for share calculation
cards.validWhen: Concentrated liquidity vaults (e.g., Gamma on Algebra/Uniswap V3) calculate LP share amounts based on the current spot price of the underlying pool.

cards.title: 4. Defective Token Transfer Hook Price Disruption
cards.detect: Custom ERC20 tokens that call `pair.sync()` inside their `_update()` or `_transfer()` function disrupt AMM pricing mechanics.
check: VERIFY: Custom ERC20 tokens that call pair.sync() inside their _update() or _transfer() function disrupt AMM pricing mechanics.
PROVE: NGP token's _update() calls pair.sync() during transfer
cards.antipattern: NGP token's _update() calls pair.sync() during transfer
cards.validWhen: Custom ERC20 tokens that call pair.sync() inside their _update() or _transfer() function disrupt AMM pricing mechanics.

cards.title: 5. Broken Vault Mint Ratio
cards.detect: Vaults or proxy contracts with misconfigured exchange rates or missing decimal normalization can mint vastly...
check: VERIFY: Vaults or proxy contracts with misconfigured exchange rates or missing decimal normalization can mint vastly disproportionate token amounts for...
PROVE: ERC1967 proxy vault has broken exchange rate
cards.antipattern: ERC1967 proxy vault has broken exchange rate
cards.validWhen: Vaults or proxy contracts with misconfigured exchange rates or missing decimal normalization can mint vastly disproportionate token amounts for trivial deposits.

cards.title: 6. Faulty Chainlink Oracle Price Feed
cards.detect: When Chainlink oracle feeds report incorrect prices for wrapped staked tokens (e.
check: VERIFY: When Chainlink oracle feeds report incorrect prices for wrapped staked tokens (e.g., wrsETH vs wstETH), the price discrepancy between oracle-reported...
PROVE: Chainlink oracle overvalues wrsETH relative to wstETH
cards.antipattern: Chainlink oracle overvalues wrsETH relative to wstETH
cards.validWhen: When Chainlink oracle feeds report incorrect prices for wrapped staked tokens (e.g., wrsETH vs wstETH), the price discrepancy between oracle-reported value and actual...

cards.title: Root Cause Categories
cards.detect: Self-Swap Price Inflation** — Protocol allows swapping token A for token A, inflating virtual price each swap
check: VERIFY: Self-Swap Price Inflation — Protocol allows swapping token A for token A, inflating virtual price each swap
cards.validWhen: Self-Swap Price Inflation — Protocol allows swapping token A for token A, inflating virtual price each swap

cards.title: Vulnerable Pattern Examples
cards.detect: swapExactTokenForToken allows tokenIn == tokenOut
check: VERIFY: swapExactTokenForToken has no check that tokenIn != tokenOut.
PROVE: Cream uses yUSD vault's pricePerShare() to value crYUSD collateral
cards.antipattern: Cream uses yUSD vault's pricePerShare() to value crYUSD collateral
cards.validWhen: swapExactTokenForToken has no check that tokenIn != tokenOut.

cards.title: Attack Scenarios
cards.detect: Attacker takes flash loan of large amounts of base or quote token
check: VERIFY: Attacker takes flash loan of large amounts of base or quote token
cards.validWhen: Attacker takes flash loan of large amounts of base or quote token

cards.title: Vulnerable Pattern Examples
cards.detect: uses live pool reserves — manipulable via flash loan in same tx
check: VERIFY: uses live pool reserves — manipulable via flash loan in same tx
PROVE: only one pool needs to be manipulated to affect minPrice/maxPrice
cards.antipattern: only one pool needs to be manipulated to affect minPrice/maxPrice
cards.validWhen: uses live pool reserves — manipulable via flash loan in same tx

cards.title: Flash Loan Oracle Manipulation
cards.detect: Protocols that derive prices from on-chain state (AMM reserves, vault share prices, LP token values) without adequate...
check: VERIFY: Protocols that derive prices from on-chain state (AMM reserves, vault share prices, LP token values) without adequate protection against flash loan...
PROVE: Flash loan large amount of token A; Swap token A for token B in AMM pool, draining B reserves
FALSIFY: Block same-transaction price reads after modifications
IMPACT: Incorrect pricing: Assets valued at manipulated rates
**Oracle Source**: Is price from manipulable on-chain source (reserves, balances)?
**TWAP Usage**: If TWAP, is window long enough (>30 min)?
cards.antipattern: Rebalancing triggered at specific price points
cards.securePattern: Block same-transaction price reads after modifications
cards.validWhen: Protocols that derive prices from on-chain state (AMM reserves, vault share prices, LP token values) without adequate protection against flash loan manipulation are...
cards.invalidWhen: Block same-transaction price reads after modifications
cards.impact: Incorrect pricing: Assets valued at manipulated rates

cards.title: Vulnerable Pattern Examples
cards.detect: No staleness validation at all
check: VERIFY: No staleness validation at all
PROVE: Exchange rate calculation with potentially stale price
cards.antipattern: Exchange rate calculation with potentially stale price
cards.validWhen: No staleness validation at all

cards.title: Vulnerable Pattern Examples
cards.detect: Raw price without exponent scaling
check: VERIFY: Raw price without exponent scaling
PROVE: Factor can be negative, causing SafeCast revert
cards.antipattern: Factor can be negative, causing SafeCast revert
cards.validWhen: Raw price without exponent scaling

cards.title: 4. Same-Transaction Price Manipulation
cards.detect: The protocol doesn't enforce that the same price is used throughout a transaction, or allows users to update prices...
check: VERIFY: protocol doesn't enforce that the same price is used throughout a transaction, or allows users to update prices between operations.
PROVE: Attacker collects two valid Pyth price signatures at different timestamps; In a single transaction: update to price1 → deposit/open position → update to price2 →...
FALSIFY: Only trusted keepers can execute LP operations
IMPACT: Protocol's oracle can return different prices in same transaction
Check if same price is used for paired operations
Verify presence of block-based price caching
cards.antipattern: Swap without price update, followed by swap with update
cards.securePattern: Only trusted keepers can execute LP operations
cards.validWhen: The protocol doesn't enforce that the same price is used throughout a transaction, or allows users to update prices between operations.
cards.invalidWhen: Only trusted keepers can execute LP operations
cards.impact: Protocol's oracle can return different prices in same transaction

cards.title: 5. Pull-Based Oracle Exploitation
cards.detect: Anyone can call `updatePriceFeeds()` with any valid (signed) price that's newer than the current on-chain price.
check: VERIFY: Anyone can call updatePriceFeeds() with any valid (signed) price that's newer than the current on-chain price.
PROVE: Attacker monitors off-chain Pyth prices for favorable historical values; Attacker finds a valid signed price that benefits their position
FALSIFY: Only accept prices newer than last committed
IMPACT: User controls which price is used for their operations
Verify price updates cannot be skipped with empty array
Check for publishTime ordering enforcement
cards.antipattern: User chooses when to update and which price to use
cards.securePattern: Only accept prices newer than last committed
cards.validWhen: Anyone can call updatePriceFeeds() with any valid (signed) price that's newer than the current on-chain price.
cards.invalidWhen: Only accept prices newer than last committed
cards.impact: User controls which price is used for their operations

cards.title: 7. Self-Liquidation Attacks
cards.detect: The ability to update prices on-demand allows attackers to create positions at one price and immediately liquidate at...
check: VERIFY: ability to update prices on-demand allows attackers to create positions at one price and immediately liquidate at another, profiting from the price...
PROVE: Find collateral/debt pair with significant historical price volatility; Flash loan collateral, open max leverage position at current price
FALSIFY: Minimum time between position open and liquidation
Check for minimum position age before liquidation
Verify price consistency within transactions/blocks
LOOK FOR: Open and close operations in same transaction possible
cards.antipattern: Position can be opened and liquidated in same tx
cards.securePattern: Minimum time between position open and liquidation
cards.validWhen: The ability to update prices on-demand allows attackers to create positions at one price and immediately liquidate at another, profiting from the price difference minus...
cards.invalidWhen: Minimum time between position open and liquidation

cards.title: 8. Timestamp Validation Vulnerabilities
cards.detect: Uses < instead of >=, logic is inverted
check: VERIFY: Uses < instead of >=, logic is inverted
PROVE: Inverted comparison operators (< instead of >=)
